tldr: Move from Countly's self-hostable analytics backend to Avo's governance layer — note these solve different problems and you may still need a destination tool.
why_switch: Avo enforces a tracking plan, improving data quality across whatever destination you use
Reduces ad-hoc event sprawl that often happens with flexible backends like Countly
Avo has a freemium tier for evaluating governance workflows
Decouples instrumentation from any one analytics tool
Works alongside (not necessarily instead of) Countly or another backend
what_to_export: List of events and their property schemas from Countly
Custom event definitions and segment rules
Saved views, funnels, retention reports (for reference)
Historical event data (if you self-host, you already control the database)
export_method: If self-hosted, you can dump the underlying database directly; if using Countly Cloud, use the documented export options. Document each event and property as the schema input for Avo. Consult Countly's official docs for current export procedures.
import_method: Set up an Avo workspace and define the tracking plan from the exported Countly schema. Connect Avo to your chosen analytics destination(s) and integrate Avo's Inspector/Codegen per the official Avo docs so engineering instruments events to spec.
gotchas: Avo isn't a Countly replacement; you'll still need a destination for analysis
If self-hosting was the reason for Countly, Avo is SaaS — verify that's acceptable
Curate which events to bring forward; don't lift-and-shift everything
Plan engineering time for SDK swap to whatever destination you pick
Decommission Countly only after the new pipeline is verified end-to-end
Historical Countly data stays in your DB; it won't move into Avo

email.subject: Countly → Avo plan
email.body:
Team,

We're introducing Avo as our analytics governance layer. Note Avo doesn't replace an analytics backend — it enforces event schema and emits to whatever destination we choose.

I'll export the current Countly event schema, draft the initial Avo tracking plan, and circulate for review. Engineering will then integrate Avo into the codebase. We'll keep Countly running until the new pipeline is verified.

Flag any Countly events or reports you depend on.

Thanks
